expectations.text: Confirms CSRD applies citing non-EU company threshold (>€150M EU turnover)
expectations.evidence: Confirms CSRD applies to non-EU parent citing €150M threshold

expectations.text: Identifies German subsidiary qualifies as a large EU company
expectations.evidence: Identifies German subsidiary as large with 600 employees

expectations.text: States first mandatory reporting year is FY 2028 (published 2029)
expectations.evidence: Incorrectly suggests German subsidiary reports starting FY2025 (confusing subsidiary vs parent obligation)

expectations.text: Explains EU subsidiary must file ESRS-compliant reports
expectations.evidence: Correctly explains subsidiaries must file under CSRD

expectations.text: Notes equivalence condition — US frameworks not yet recognised as equivalent
expectations.evidence: Does not address equivalence status of US frameworks
